run.command: cursor-agent --print --output-format stream-json --force --trust --workspace <target-root> "<first-run prompt: scan the target, then report success/component+surface counts/one risk>"

exact_outcome: Authenticated Cursor Agent CLI run completed in 24.4s. The agent ran the Portolan scan command correctly, inspected the produced bundle (system-map.json + gaps), and gave a meaningful 3-line report: scan succeeded (receipt status: completed), system-map.json has 1 component and 0 surfaces, risk/unknown = gap-deps (dependencies not assessed, no Syft/CycloneDX producer). Earlier 11s probe was simply too short; the lane is fully authenticated and operational from the terminal.

scorecard_fields.prompt_used: first-run prompt: run portolan-scan.sh on the target into <target>/.portolan/atlas, then report (1) scan success, (2) component+surface counts in system-map.json, (3) one risk or unknown
scorecard_fields.target_root: isolated temp git repo with 2 sub-repos (api-svc go module + web-client node package)
scorecard_fields.ui_opened_or_handoff: not_applicable (headless --print run; viewer handoff verified separately by harness-viewer-system-map-smoke.sh)
scorecard_fields.first_useful_explanation: '1. Success: Yes — scan completed with exit code 0 ... 2. Counts: system-map.json has 1 component and 0 surfaces (0 relationships). 3. Risk/unknown: Dependencies were not assessed — no Syft/CycloneDX producer output, so the dependency graph is unknown (unknown:gap-deps).'
scorecard_fields.target_source_modified_note: only .portolan/ created; source tree unchanged (git status clean except untracked .portolan/)
